# Source Han Serif SC -- On-demand font subsets

This directory contains the **pre-split** Source Han Serif SC font subsets
generated by `cn-font-split`. The full font (~17MB) is split into small
woff2 chunks so only visible characters load on the first screen (<2MB).

## Directory structure

```
source-han-serif-sc/
  result.css          -- Generated CSS with @font-face rules (DO NOT EDIT)
  *.woff2             -- Subset chunks (auto-generated, DO NOT EDIT)
  preview.png         -- Font preview image (optional)
  source/             -- [gitignored] Original font file lives here
    SourceHanSerifSC-Regular.otf   -- Place the source OTF here
```

## Initial setup

1. Download the font from the official Adobe release:
   https://github.com/adobe-fonts/source-han-serif/releases

2. Extract `SourceHanSerifSC-Regular.otf` from the zip and place it in
   `public/fonts/source-han-serif-sc/source/`.

3. Run the split script:

   ```bash
   bun run scripts/split-fonts.ts
   ```

4. The script generates `result.css` + woff2 chunks in this directory.

## How it works

- `cn-font-split` reads the full OTF/TTF and splits it by Unicode ranges
  into woff2 chunks (~70KB each).
- `result.css` contains `@font-face` rules that use `unicode-range` to
  load only the chunks matching visible characters.
- `globals.css` imports `result.css` via `@import url(...)`.
- `font-display: swap` ensures text is visible immediately with a fallback
  font, then swaps to the web font once loaded.

## Regenerating

After changing the source font file, re-run:

```bash
bun run scripts/split-fonts.ts
```

This overwrites `result.css` and all woff2 files.
